targets: Iranian S-300 air defense systems near Isfahan
description: Calibrated retaliatory strike after Iran's first direct attack on Israel. Widely reported as a limited message strike rather than escalation.

operation: Israel retaliatory strikes — air defense & missiles
targets: Iranian air defense systems, ballistic missile production and storage sites
description: Largest Israeli direct attack on Iran at the time, following Iran's 180-missile barrage. Focus on degrading AD and missile industrial capacity.

location: Tehran, Isfahan, Natanz, military sites across Iran
targets: Nuclear facilities, missile sites, IRGC command, nuclear scientists (targeted killings)
description: Israel opened the Twelve-Day War with surprise deep strikes across Iran's nuclear and military infrastructure.

operation: Twelve-Day War — US joins Israeli air campaign
targets: Iranian military infrastructure supporting ongoing Israeli campaign
description: US forces joined Israeli air operations during the Twelve-Day War as Iran and Houthis continued striking Israel.

operation: US B-2 strikes — Fordow enrichment plant
description: B-2 sorties reportedly used massive penetrator munitions against deeply buried Fordow nuclear site during Twelve-Day War.
sourceNote: B-2 not in OSINT coalition_aircraft.json; munition attribution from public reporting, not DoD manifest.

description: Concurrent US strike package against Natanz as part of coordinated nuclear-site campaign with Israel.

operation: US strikes — Isfahan nuclear technology center
targets: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center / conversion facilities
description: Third pillar of reported US nuclear-site strike package during Twelve-Day War ceasefire push.

targets: Leadership compounds, IRGC HQ, missile storage, air defenses; ~900 strikes in 12 hours
description: Joint US-Israel opening campaign. Khamenei assassinated. Strikes launched during active Geneva negotiations.
sourceNote: Per-sortie ordnance breakdown not released; weapons inferred from platform loadouts and standard US SEAD/strike packages.

operation: Epic Fury — mobile TEL / missile launcher hunt
description: CENTCOM: Epic Fury 'laser-focused on destroying Iranian offensive missiles' — hunting mobile launchers as Iran continued TP4 retaliation.

operation: Epic Fury — SEAD / air defense suppression
targets: S-300, Bavar-373, and radar networks protecting missile and nuclear sites
description: Follow-on SEAD campaign to degrade Iranian IADS enabling subsequent strike packages as IRGC continued True Promise 4.

targets: Missile production, solid-fuel motor facilities, storage depots
description: Strikes on Iran's missile industrial base to reduce replenishment capacity during sustained exchange.
sources: Pattern of prior Israeli targeting of Parchin/Khojir complexes

operation: Epic Fury — naval / coastal strike package
location: Bandar Abbas / Strait of Hormuz approaches, Iran
targets: IRIN/IRGCN coastal facilities, radar, and missile batteries threatening shipping
description: US strikes on coastal infrastructure as Iran closed Strait of Hormuz and targeted Gulf shipping.

location: Persian Gulf / Kharg Island area, Iran
targets: Iranian energy export infrastructure (threatened, not confirmed struck)
description: Trump threatened extensive attacks on Iranian energy sites if Hormuz not reopened. Troop surge ordered; strike status unresolved in dataset.
sourceNote: Threat/ultimatum — not confirmed executed as of dataset cutoff.
